purpose: Maximum visual quality mountain megawall source list. Use manual downloads for final commercial production; the included procedural textures are placeholders.
license_note: Poly Haven and ambientCG downloadable assets are CC0. Check each non-CC0 reference manually. Do not rip Google/Yandex/Bing map tiles.

hdri_lighting.why: 24K overcast mountain/hill HDRI, good cold low-contrast light for bleak atmosphere.
hdri_lighting.recommended_download: EXR 16K or 24K for Blender lighting, not for runtime Godot sky unless optimized.

hdri_lighting.why: 24K mist/fog HDRI, useful for mountain haze lighting reference.
hdri_lighting.recommended_download: EXR 16K/24K for Blender render, 2K/4K tonemapped for Godot sky preview.

hdri_lighting.why: CC0 29K EXR HDRIs for extra lighting options.
hdri_lighting.recommended_download: Use for Blender lighting; downsample for runtime.

polyhaven_pbr.why: 16K cliff material; strong for eroded mountain wall render.
polyhaven_pbr.recommended_download: 8K/16K JPG/PNG maps for Blender; export baked mountain cards to Godot.

polyhaven_pbr.why: 16K weathered rock; good for high-frequency mountain face details.
8K dark grey-brown rock; good for cold night silhouettes.
8K rough stones/gravel/dirt, useful for foreground ridge and foothills.
8K soft powdery snow, cleaner for high peaks.
8K rough snow, useful as secondary snow detail/noise.

dem_sources.why: Real mountain shape. Use Khan Tengri / Jengish Chokusu / Inylchek Glacier bbox.
dem_sources.note: May require free OpenTopography API key for automated API export. Manual website export is easiest.

dem_sources.why: Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF DEM tiles. Better for technical/GIS pipeline.
Fallback global DEM source if Copernicus is inconvenient.

reference_search_terms: Jengish Chokusu Pik Pobeda Inylchek Glacier

final_output_targets.production_max: 16384x4096 or 32768x8192 offline render split into 4096 tiles
